content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April
14,
1992
Philip R. Recht
Manatt,
Phelps, Phillips
& Kantor
11355 West Olympic Blvd;
Los Angeles,
CA
90064—1614
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-92-193
Dear Mr.
Recht:
-
- This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).’
Because you have not
provided the identity of the person or persons on whose behalf you
are seeking assistance,
we consider your request to be one for
informal assistance.
2
Your letter, dated March 25,
1992,
accurately summarizes my
advice that a campaign committee whose principal activity is sup
porting a local ballot measure may include in its name the phrase
“Yes on Charter Amendment F” as opposed to including in its name
the phrase
“Yes on Proposition F.”
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Sandra L.
Taylor
Political Reform Consultant
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All. statu.tory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Sec
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
4281 Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916) 322-66’

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a campaign committee supporting a local ballot measure may include the phrase 'Yes on Charter Amendment F' in its name rather than 'Yes on Proposition F' under the Political Reform Act's campaign disclosure provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that a campaign committee whose principal activity is supporting a local ballot measure may include in its name the phrase 'Yes on Charter Amendment F' as opposed to 'Yes on Proposition F.'
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; applicant identity not disclosed; document lacks traditional section headers but contains clear advice regarding campaign committee naming conventions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a campaign committee supporting a local ballot measure may include the phrase 'Yes on Charter Amendment F' in its name rather than 'Yes on Proposition F' under the Political Reform Act's campaign disclosure provisions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC confirms that a campaign committee whose principal activity is supporting a local ballot measure may include in its name the phrase 'Yes on Charter Amendment F' as opposed to 'Yes on Proposition F.'
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission April 14, 1992 Philip R. Recht Manatt, Phelps, Phillips & Kantor 11355 West Olympic Blvd; Los Angeles, CA 90064—1614 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. 1-92-193 Dear Mr. Recht: - - This is in response to your request for confirmation of telephone advice regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’ Because you have not provided the identity of the person or persons on whose behalf you are seeking assistance, we consider your request to be one for informal assistance. 2 Your letter, dated March 25, 1992, accurately summarizes my advice that a campaign committee whose principal activity is sup porting a local ballot measure may include in its name the phrase “Yes on Charter Amendment F” as opposed to including in its name the phrase “Yes on Proposition F.” If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Sandra L. Taylor Political Reform Consultant 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All. statu.tory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).) 4281 Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-66’
embedding.summary: FPPC responds to an informal assistance request confirming that a campaign committee supporting a local ballot measure may use 'Yes on Charter Amendment F' in its name rather than 'Yes on Proposition F' under the Political Reform Act.
